# UC-10.12.22 · Infusion Pump Connectivity Monitoring

## Description

Pump disconnects can delay therapy; supports Joint Commission device management expectations.

## Value

Pump disconnects can delay therapy; supports Joint Commission device management expectations.

## Implementation

Integrate with CMMS for maintenance; alert biomed on extended silence from critical care beds.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Medical device integration (MDI), RTLS.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype="mdi:pump"`, `sourcetype="hl7:mdt"`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Integrate with CMMS for maintenance; alert biomed on extended silence from critical care beds.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=clinical sourcetype="mdi:pump"
| stats latest(_time) as last_seen, latest(alarm_state) as alarm by pump_id, unit
| eval gap_sec=now()-last_seen
| where gap_sec>300 OR alarm="AIR_IN_LINE"
| table pump_id, unit, gap_sec, alarm
```

Understanding this SPL

**Infusion Pump Connectivity Monitoring** — Pump disconnects can delay therapy; supports Joint Commission device management expectations.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype="mdi:pump"`, `sourcetype="hl7:mdt"`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Medical device integration (MDI), RTLS. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: clinical; **sourcetype**: mdi:pump. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=clinical, sourcetype="mdi:pump". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by pump_id, unit** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **gap_sec** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where gap_sec>300 OR alarm="AIR_IN_LINE"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Pipeline stage (see **Infusion Pump Connectivity Monitoring**): table pump_id, unit, gap_sec, alarm


Step 3 — Validate
Run the search on recent production data, spot-check a handful of results against the source system or operational runbooks (cash operations, EHR, payment gateway, etc.), and adjust thresholds to match your seasonal patterns and test windows so teams are not flooded with noise.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Status map (units), Table (stale pumps), Timeline (alarms).

## SPL

```spl
index=clinical sourcetype="mdi:pump"
| stats latest(_time) as last_seen, latest(alarm_state) as alarm by pump_id, unit
| eval gap_sec=now()-last_seen
| where gap_sec>300 OR alarm="AIR_IN_LINE"
| table pump_id, unit, gap_sec, alarm
```

Status map (units), Table (stale pumps), Timeline (alarms).
